NAME

crypto_handshake_context_t g_crypto_ctx = {0}

Global H.265 encoder context for client video streaming.

Detailed Description

🌐 Client connection manager: TCP connection, reconnection with exponential backoff, and thread-safe transmission

The connection management follows a robust state machine:

1.
Initialization: Socket creation and address resolution
2.
Connection: TCP handshake with configurable timeout
3.
Capability Exchange: Send terminal capabilities and client info
4.
Active Monitoring: Health checks and keepalive management
5.
Disconnection: Graceful or forced connection teardown
6.
Reconnection: Exponential backoff retry logic

Thread Safety

All packet transmission functions use a global send mutex to prevent interleaved packets on the wire. The socket file descriptor is protected with atomic operations for thread-safe access across multiple threads.

Reconnection Strategy

Implements exponential backoff with jitter:

  • Initial delay: 10ms
  • Exponential growth: delay = 10ms + (200ms * attempt)
  • Maximum delay: 5 seconds
  • Jitter: Small random component to prevent thundering herd

Platform Compatibility

Uses platform abstraction layer for:

  • Socket creation and management (Winsock vs BSD sockets)
  • Network error handling and error string conversion
  • Address resolution and connection timeout handling
  • Socket options (keepalive, nodelay)

Integration Points

  • main.c: Calls connection establishment and monitoring functions
  • protocol.c: Uses thread-safe send functions for packet transmission
  • keepalive.c: Monitors connection health and triggers reconnection
  • capture.c: Sends media data through connection
  • audio.c: Sends audio data through connection

Error Handling

Connection errors are classified into:

  • Temporary: Network congestion, temporary DNS failures (retry)
  • Permanent: Invalid address, authentication failure (report and exit)
  • Timeout: Connection establishment timeout (retry with backoff)
  • Loss: Existing connection broken (immediate reconnection attempt)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

Version

2.0

Function Documentation

void server_connection_cleanup ()

Cleanup connection management subsystem. Closes any active connection and destroys synchronization objects. Called during client shutdown.

Definition at line 1107 of file src/client/server.c.

1108   if (!GET_OPTION(quiet)) {
1109     log_set_terminal_output(true);
1110   }
1111   server_connection_close();
1112   mutex_destroy(&g_send_mutex);
1113 }

void
  server_connection_close ()


Close the server connection gracefully. Close server connection
    gracefully.




Marks connection as inactive and closes the socket. This function
    is safe to call multiple times and from multiple threads.


Definition at line 956 of file
  src/client/server.c.


956                                {
957   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] server_connection_close() called");
958 
959   // Acquire send mutex to prevent race with threaded_send_image_frame()
960   // This ensures any in-flight sends complete before we destroy the transport
961   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
962   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
963 
964   // Destroy ACIP transport before closing socket
965   if (g_client_transport) {
966     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Destroying transport=%p (is_connected=%s) before closing socket",
967               (void *)g_client_transport, acip_transport_is_connected(g_client_transport) ? "true" : "false");
968     acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
969     g_client_transport = NULL;
970     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Transport destroyed, g_client_transport set to NULL");
971   }
972 
973   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
974 
975   if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
976     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Closing socket: %d", (int)g_sockfd);
977     close_socket(g_sockfd);
978     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
979     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Socket closed");
980   }
981 
982   g_my_client_id = 0;
983 
984   // Cleanup crypto context if encryption was enabled
985   if (g_encryption_enabled) {
986     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Cleaning up crypto context");
987     crypto_handshake_destroy(&g_crypto_ctx);
988     g_encryption_enabled = false;
989   }
990 
991   // Turn ON terminal logging when connection is closed (unless it was disabled with --quiet)
992   if (!GET_OPTION(quiet)) {
993     log_set_terminal_output(true);
994   }
995   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] server_connection_close() completed");
996 }

int
  server_connection_establish (const char * address, int port, int
  reconnect_attempt, bool first_connection, bool
  has_ever_connected)


Establish connection to ascii-chat server. Attempts to connect to
    the specified server with full capability negotiation. Implements
    reconnection logic with exponential backoff for failed attempts. On
    successful connection, performs initial handshake including terminal
    capabilities and client join protocol.




Parameters


address Server IP address or hostname


port Server port number


reconnect_attempt Current reconnection attempt number (0 for first)


first_connection True if this is the initial connection attempt


has_ever_connected True if a connection was ever successfully
  established


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 353 of file
  src/client/server.c.


354                                                          {
355   (void)first_connection; // Currently unused
356   if (!address || port <= 0) {
357     log_error("Invalid address or port parameters");
358     return -1;
359   }
360 
361   // Close any existing connection
362   if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
363     close_socket(g_sockfd);
364     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
365   }
366 
367   // Apply reconnection delay if this is a retry
368   if (reconnect_attempt > 0) {
369     unsigned int delay_us = get_reconnect_delay(reconnect_attempt);
370     // Reconnection attempt logged only to file
371     platform_sleep_us(delay_us);
372 
373     // Check if user requested exit during reconnection delay
374     if (should_exit()) {
375       log_debug("Exit requested during reconnection delay");
376       return -1;
377     }
378   } else {
379     // Initial connection logged only to file
380   }
381 
382   // Check for WebSocket URL - handle separately from TCP
383   if (url_is_websocket(address)) {
384     // WebSocket connection - bypass TCP socket creation
385     // Use the original address (URL already contains port if specified)
386     const char *ws_url = address;
387 
388     // Parse for debug logging
389     url_parts_t url_parts = {0};
390     if (url_parse(address, &url_parts) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
391       log_info("Connecting via WebSocket: %s (scheme=%s, host=%s, port=%d)", ws_url, url_parts.scheme, url_parts.host,
392                url_parts.port);
393 
394       // Set server IP and port for crypto handshake
395       // This ensures the crypto context has proper server address info for known_hosts verification
396       server_connection_set_ip(url_parts.host);
397       log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Set server IP to %s from WebSocket URL", url_parts.host);
398     } else {
399       log_info("Connecting via WebSocket: %s", ws_url);
400     }
401 
402     // Initialize crypto if encryption is enabled
403     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Calling client_crypto_init()");
404     if (client_crypto_init() != 0) {
405       log_error("Failed to initialize crypto (password required or incorrect)");
406       log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_init() failed");
407       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
408       return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
409     }
410 
411     // Get crypto context for transport
412     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_client_is_ready() ? crypto_client_get_context() : NULL;
413 
414     // Create WebSocket transport (handles connection internally)
415     g_client_transport = acip_websocket_client_transport_create("client", ws_url, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
416     if (!g_client_transport) {
417       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket ACIP transport");
418       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
419       return -1;
420     }
421     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Created WebSocket ACIP transport with crypto context");
422 
423     // Set connection as active
424     atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, true);
425     atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, false);
426 
427     // CRITICAL: Wait for WebSocket connection to be established before sending
428     // The service thread is running but may not have completed TLS handshake yet
429     // Don't block indefinitely - use timeout loops to allow responsiveness
430     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Waiting for WebSocket connection to be established...");
431     const int CONNECT_CHECK_ATTEMPTS = 300; // 300 * 50ms = 15 seconds timeout
432     int connect_attempts = 0;
433     while (!acip_transport_is_connected(g_client_transport) && connect_attempts < CONNECT_CHECK_ATTEMPTS) {
434       platform_sleep_us(50000); // Sleep 50ms between checks
435       connect_attempts++;
436       if (connect_attempts % 20 == 0) { // Log every 1 second (20 * 50ms)
437         log_info("CLIENT_CONNECT: Waiting for connection... (%d seconds elapsed)", connect_attempts / 20);
438       }
439     }
440 
441     if (!acip_transport_is_connected(g_client_transport)) {
442       log_error("Failed to establish WebSocket connection within 15 seconds");
443       acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
444       g_client_transport = NULL;
445       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
446       atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
447       return -1;
448     }
449 
450     log_info("CLIENT_CONNECT: WebSocket connection established, proceeding with handshake");
451 
452     // Send initial terminal capabilities to server
453     int result = threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect((int)terminal_get_effective_width(),
454                                                               (int)terminal_get_effective_height());
455     if (result < 0) {
456       log_error("Failed to send initial capabilities to server: %s", network_error_string());
457       acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
458       g_client_transport = NULL;
459       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
460       return -1;
461     }
462 
463     // Disable terminal logging after initial setup (for non-snapshot mode)
464     if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && has_ever_connected) {
465       log_set_terminal_output(false);
466     }
467 
468     // Build capabilities flags
469     uint32_t my_capabilities = CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO;
470     log_debug("GET_OPTION(audio_enabled) = %d (sending CLIENT_JOIN)", GET_OPTION(audio_enabled));
471     if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
472       log_debug("Adding CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO to capabilities");
473       my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO;
474     }
475     if (GET_OPTION(color_mode) != COLOR_MODE_NONE) {
476       my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_COLOR;
477     }
478     if (GET_OPTION(stretch)) {
479       my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH;
480     }
481 
482     // Generate display name from username + PID
483     const char *display_name = platform_get_username();
484     char my_display_name[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
485     int pid = getpid();
486     SAFE_SNPRINTF(my_display_name, sizeof(my_display_name), "%s-%d", display_name, pid);
487     if (threaded_send_client_join_packet(my_display_name, my_capabilities) < 0) {
488       log_error("Failed to send client join packet: %s", network_error_string());
489       acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
490       g_client_transport = NULL;
491       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
492       return -1;
493     }
494 
495     log_info("WebSocket connection established successfully");
496     url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
497     return 0;
498   }
499 
500   // Resolve server address using getaddrinfo() for IPv4/IPv6 support
501   // Special handling for localhost: ensure we try both IPv6 (::1) and IPv4 (127.0.0.1)
502   // Many systems only map "localhost" to 127.0.0.1 in /etc/hosts
503   bool is_localhost = (strcmp(address, "localhost") == 0 || is_localhost_ipv4(address) || is_localhost_ipv6(address));
504 
505   struct addrinfo hints, *res = NULL, *addr_iter;
506   memset(&hints, 0, sizeof(hints));
507   hints.ai_family = AF_UNSPEC; // Allow IPv4 or IPv6
508   hints.ai_socktype = SOCK_STREAM;
509   if (is_localhost) {
510     hints.ai_flags = AI_NUMERICSERV; // Optimize for localhost
511   }
512 
513   char port_str[16];
514   SAFE_SNPRINTF(port_str, sizeof(port_str), "%d", port);
515 
516   // For localhost, try IPv6 loopback (::1) first, then fall back to DNS resolution
517   if (is_localhost) {
518     log_debug("Localhost detected - trying IPv6 loopback [::1]:%s first...", port_str);
519     hints.ai_family = AF_INET6;
520     hints.ai_flags = AI_NUMERICHOST | AI_NUMERICSERV;
521 
522     int ipv6_result = getaddrinfo("::1", port_str, &hints, &res);
523     if (ipv6_result == 0 && res != NULL) {
524       // Try IPv6 loopback connection
525       g_sockfd = socket_create("ipv6", res->ai_family, res->ai_socktype, res->ai_protocol);
526       if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
527         log_debug("Trying IPv6 loopback connection to [::1]:%s...", port_str);
528         if (connect_with_timeout(g_sockfd, res->ai_addr, res->ai_addrlen, CONNECT_TIMEOUT)) {
529           log_debug("Connection successful using IPv6 loopback");
530           SAFE_STRNCPY(g_server_ip, "::1", sizeof(g_server_ip));
531           freeaddrinfo(res);
532           res = NULL; // Prevent double-free at connection_success label
533           goto connection_success;
534         }
535         if (socket_get_error(g_sockfd) != 0) {
536           log_debug("NETWORK_ERROR: %d", (int)socket_get_error(g_sockfd));
537         } else {
538           // log_debug("IPv6 loopback connection failed: %s", network_error_string());
539         }
540         close_socket(g_sockfd);
541         g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
542       }
543       freeaddrinfo(res);
544       res = NULL;
545     }
546 
547     // Check if user requested exit (Ctrl-C) before trying IPv4
548     if (should_exit()) {
549       log_debug("Exit requested during connection attempt");
550       return -1;
551     }
552 
553     // IPv6 failed, try IPv4 loopback (127.0.0.1)
554     log_debug("IPv6 failed, trying IPv4 loopback 127.0.0.1:%s...", port_str);
555     hints.ai_family = AF_INET;
556 
557     int ipv4_result = getaddrinfo("127.0.0.1", port_str, &hints, &res);
558     if (ipv4_result == 0 && res != NULL) {
559       g_sockfd = socket_create("ipv4", res->ai_family, res->ai_socktype, res->ai_protocol);
560       if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
561         log_debug("Trying IPv4 loopback connection to 127.0.0.1:%s...", port_str);
562         if (connect_with_timeout(g_sockfd, res->ai_addr, res->ai_addrlen, CONNECT_TIMEOUT)) {
563           log_debug("Connection successful using IPv4 loopback");
564           SAFE_STRNCPY(g_server_ip, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(g_server_ip));
565           freeaddrinfo(res);
566           res = NULL; // Prevent double-free at connection_success label
567           goto connection_success;
568         }
569         if (socket_get_error(g_sockfd) != 0) {
570           log_debug("NETWORK_ERROR: %d", (int)socket_get_error(g_sockfd));
571         } else {
572           // log_debug("IPv4 loopback connection failed: %s", network_error_string());
573         }
574         close_socket(g_sockfd);
575         g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
576       }
577       freeaddrinfo(res);
578       res = NULL;
579     }
580 
581     // Both IPv6 and IPv4 loopback failed for localhost
582     log_warn("Could not connect to localhost using either IPv6 or IPv4 loopback");
583     return -1;
584   }
585 
586   // For non-localhost addresses, use standard resolution
587   log_debug("Resolving server address '%s' port %s...", address, port_str);
588   hints.ai_family = AF_UNSPEC;
589   hints.ai_flags = 0;
590   int getaddr_result = getaddrinfo(address, port_str, &hints, &res);
591   if (getaddr_result != 0) {
592     log_error("Failed to resolve server address '%s': %s", address, gai_strerror(getaddr_result));
593     return -1;
594   }
595 
596   // Try each address returned by getaddrinfo() until one succeeds
597   // Prefer IPv6 over IPv4: try IPv6 addresses first, then fall back to IPv4
598   for (int address_family = AF_INET6; address_family >= AF_INET; address_family -= (AF_INET6 - AF_INET)) {
599     for (addr_iter = res; addr_iter != NULL; addr_iter = addr_iter->ai_next) {
600       // Skip addresses that don't match current pass (IPv6 first, then IPv4)
601       if (addr_iter->ai_family != address_family) {
602         continue;
603       }
604 
605       // Create socket with appropriate address family
606       const char *socket_name =
607           (addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET6) ? "client_socket_server_ipv6" : "client_socket_server_ipv4";
608       g_sockfd = socket_create(socket_name, addr_iter->ai_family, addr_iter->ai_socktype, addr_iter->ai_protocol);
609       if (g_sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
610         log_debug("Could not create socket for address family %d: %s", addr_iter->ai_family, network_error_string());
611         continue; // Try next address
612       }
613 
614       // Log which address family we're trying
615       if (addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET) {
616         log_debug("Trying IPv4 connection...");
617       } else if (addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET6) {
618         log_debug("Trying IPv6 connection...");
619       }
620 
621       // Attempt connection with timeout
622       if (connect_with_timeout(g_sockfd, addr_iter->ai_addr, addr_iter->ai_addrlen, CONNECT_TIMEOUT)) {
623         // Connection successful!
624         log_debug("Connection successful using %s", addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET    ? "IPv4"
625                                                     : addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET6 ? "IPv6"
626                                                                                        : "unknown protocol");
627 
628         // Extract server IP address for known_hosts
629         if (format_ip_address(addr_iter->ai_family, addr_iter->ai_addr, g_server_ip, sizeof(g_server_ip)) ==
630             ASCIICHAT_OK) {
631           log_debug("Resolved server IP: %s", g_server_ip);
632         } else {
633           log_warn("Failed to format server IP address");
634         }
635 
636         goto connection_success; // Break out of both loops
637       }
638 
639       // Connection failed - close socket and try next address
640       if (socket_get_error(g_sockfd) != 0) {
641         log_debug("NETWORK_ERROR: %d", (int)socket_get_error(g_sockfd));
642       } else {
643         // log_debug("Connection failed: %s", network_error_string());
644       }
645       close_socket(g_sockfd);
646       g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
647     }
648   }
649 
650 connection_success:
651 
652   if (res) {
653     freeaddrinfo(res);
654   }
655 
656   // If we exhausted all addresses without success, fail
657   if (g_sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
658     log_warn("Could not connect to server %s:%d (tried all addresses)", address, port);
659     return -1;
660   }
661 
662   // Connection successful - extract local port for client ID
663   struct sockaddr_storage local_addr = {0};
664   socklen_t addr_len = sizeof(local_addr);
665   if (getsockname(g_sockfd, (struct sockaddr *)&local_addr, &addr_len) == -1) {
666     log_error("Failed to get local socket address: %s", network_error_string());
667     close_socket(g_sockfd);
668     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
669     return -1;
670   }
671 
672   // Extract port from either IPv4 or IPv6 address
673   int local_port = 0;
674   if (((struct sockaddr *)&local_addr)->sa_family == AF_INET) {
675     local_port = NET_TO_HOST_U16(((struct sockaddr_in *)&local_addr)->sin_port);
676   } else if (((struct sockaddr *)&local_addr)->sa_family == AF_INET6) {
677     local_port = NET_TO_HOST_U16(((struct sockaddr_in6 *)&local_addr)->sin6_port);
678   }
679   g_my_client_id = (uint32_t)local_port;
680 
681   // Mark connection as active immediately after successful socket connection
682   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, true);
683   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, false);
684   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_reconnect, false);
685 
686   // Initialize crypto BEFORE starting protocol handshake
687   // Note: server IP is already set above in the connection loop
688   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Calling client_crypto_init()");
689   if (client_crypto_init() != 0) {
690     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto (password required or incorrect)");
691     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_init() failed");
692     close_socket(g_sockfd);
693     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
694     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED; // SSH key password was wrong - no retry
695   }
696 
697   // Perform crypto handshake if encryption is enabled
698   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Calling client_crypto_handshake()");
699   int handshake_result = client_crypto_handshake(g_sockfd);
700   if (handshake_result != 0) {
701     log_error("Crypto handshake failed");
702     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_handshake() failed with code %d", handshake_result);
703     close_socket(g_sockfd);
704     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
705     FATAL(ERROR_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE,
706           "Crypto handshake failed with server - this usually indicates a protocol mismatch or network issue");
707   }
708   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_handshake() succeeded");
709 
710   // Create ACIP transport for protocol-agnostic packet sending
711   // The transport wraps the socket with encryption context from the handshake
712   const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_client_is_ready() ? crypto_client_get_context() : NULL;
713 
714   // Create TCP transport (WebSocket is handled earlier in the function)
715   g_client_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("client", g_sockfd, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
716   if (!g_client_transport) {
717     log_error("Failed to create TCP ACIP transport");
718     close_socket(g_sockfd);
719     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
720     return -1;
721   }
722   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Created TCP ACIP transport with crypto context");
723 
724   // Turn OFF terminal logging when successfully connected to server
725   // First connection - we'll disable logging after main.c shows the "Connected successfully" message
726   if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
727     log_debug("Connected to server - terminal logging will be disabled after initial setup");
728   } else {
729     log_debug("Connected to server - terminal logging kept enabled for snapshot mode");
730   }
731 
732   // Configure socket options for optimal performance
733   if (socket_set_keepalive(g_sockfd, true) < 0) {
734     log_warn("Failed to set socket keepalive: %s", network_error_string());
735   }
736 
737   // Configure socket buffers and TCP_NODELAY for optimal performance
738   asciichat_error_t sock_config_result = socket_configure_buffers(g_sockfd);
739   if (sock_config_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
740     log_warn("Failed to configure socket: %s", network_error_string());
741   }
742 
743   // Send initial terminal capabilities to server (this may generate debug logs)
744   int result = threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect((int)terminal_get_effective_width(),
745                                                             (int)terminal_get_effective_height());
746   if (result < 0) {
747     log_error("Failed to send initial capabilities to server: %s", network_error_string());
748     close_socket(g_sockfd);
749     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
750     return -1;
751   }
752 
753   // Now disable terminal logging after capabilities are sent (for reconnections)
754   if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && has_ever_connected) {
755     log_set_terminal_output(false);
756     log_debug("Reconnected to server - terminal logging disabled to prevent interference with ASCII display");
757   }
758 
759   // Send client join packet for multi-user support
760   uint32_t my_capabilities = CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO; // Basic video capability
761   log_debug("GET_OPTION(audio_enabled) = %d (sending CLIENT_JOIN)", GET_OPTION(audio_enabled));
762   if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
763     log_debug("Adding CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO to capabilities");
764     my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO;
765   }
766   if (GET_OPTION(color_mode) != COLOR_MODE_NONE) {
767     my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_COLOR;
768   }
769   if (GET_OPTION(stretch)) {
770     my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH;
771   }
772 
773   // Generate display name from username + PID
774   const char *display_name = platform_get_username();
775 
776   char my_display_name[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
777   int pid = getpid();
778   SAFE_SNPRINTF(my_display_name, sizeof(my_display_name), "%s-%d", display_name, pid);
779 
780   if (threaded_send_client_join_packet(my_display_name, my_capabilities) < 0) {
781     log_error("Failed to send client join packet: %s", network_error_string());
782     close_socket(g_sockfd);
783     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
784     return -1;
785   }
786 
787   // Connection already marked as active after socket creation
788 
789   return 0;
790 }






References acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(),
    acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_store_bool(), CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO,
    CLIENT_CAP_COLOR, CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH, CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO,
    client_crypto_handshake(), client_crypto_init(),

320     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_connection_lost, "server_connection_lost_flag", NULL);

void
  server_connection_shutdown ()


Emergency connection shutdown for signal handlers. Emergency
    shutdown for signal handlers.




Performs immediate connection shutdown without waiting for
    graceful close procedures. Uses socket shutdown to interrupt any blocking
    recv() operations in other threads.


Definition at line 1043 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1043                                   {
1044   // NOTE: This function may be called from:
1045   //   - Signal handlers on Unix (async-signal-safe context)
1046   //   - SetConsoleCtrlHandler callback thread on Windows (separate thread context)
1047   // Only use atomic operations and simple system calls - NO mutex locks, NO malloc, NO logging.
1048 
1049   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
1050   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, true);
1051 
1052   if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1053     // Only shutdown() the socket to interrupt blocking recv()/send() operations.
1054     // Do NOT close() here - on Windows, closing the socket while another thread
1055     // is using it is undefined behavior and can cause STATUS_STACK_BUFFER_OVERRUN.
1056     // The actual socket close happens in server_connection_close() which is called
1057     // from the main thread after worker threads have been joined.
1058     socket_shutdown(g_sockfd, SHUT_RDWR);
1059   }
1060 
1061   // For WebSocket transport: interrupt blocking recv() calls
1062   // The recv() checks is_connected before waiting for data. Setting this to false
1063   // will cause any blocked recv() to return immediately with CONNECTION_CLOSED.
1064   // We use a function that's async-signal-safe (no mutexes) to do this.
1065   server_connection_interrupt_websocket_recv();
1066 
1067   // DO NOT call log_set_terminal_output() here - it uses mutex which is NOT async-signal-safe.
1068   // The normal cleanup path in shutdown_client() will handle logging state.
1069 }

1192

asciichat_error_t threaded_send_audio_opus (const
  uint8_t * opus_data, size_t opus_size, int sample_rate, int
  frame_duration)


Thread-safe Opus audio frame transmission. Sends a single
    Opus-encoded audio frame to the server with proper synchronization and
    encryption support.




Parameters


opus_data Opus-encoded audio data


opus_size Size of encoded frame


sample_rate Sample rate in Hz


frame_duration Frame duration in milliseconds


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1225 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1226                                                                {
1227   // Get transport reference while holding mutex (brief lock)
1228   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1229 
1230   // Check connection status and get transport reference
1231   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) || !g_client_transport) {
1232     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1233     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection not active or transport unavailable");
1234   }
1235 
1236   // Get transport reference - transport has its own internal synchronization
1237   acip_transport_t *transport = g_client_transport;
1238   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1239 
1240   // Build Opus packet with header (outside mutex - no blocking I/O yet)
1241   size_t header_size = 16; // sample_rate (4), frame_duration (4), reserved (8)
1242   size_t total_size = header_size + opus_size;
1243   void *packet_data = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, total_size);
1244   if (!packet_data) {
1245     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate buffer for Opus packet: %zu bytes", total_size);
1246   }
1247 
1248   // Write header in network byte order
1249   uint8_t *buf = (uint8_t *)packet_data;
1250   uint32_t sr = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)sample_rate);
1251   uint32_t fd = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)frame_duration);
1252   memcpy(buf, &sr, 4);
1253   memcpy(buf + 4, &fd, 4);
1254   memset(buf + 8, 0, 8); // Reserved
1255 
1256   // Copy Opus data
1257   memcpy(buf + header_size, opus_data, opus_size);
1258 
1259   // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex to prevent deadlock on TCP buffer full
1260   asciichat_error_t result =
1261       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH, packet_data, total_size, 0);
1262 
1263   // Clean up
1264   buffer_pool_free(NULL, packet_data, total_size);
1265 
1266   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1267   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1268     server_connection_lost();
1269     return result;
1270   }
1271 
1272   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1273 }

    PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH, server_connection_lost(), and

1578   join_packet.client_id = HOST_TO_NET_U32(0); // Will be assigned by server
1579   SAFE_SNPRINTF(join_packet.display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN, "%s", display_name ? display_name : "Unknown");
1580   join_packet.capabilities = HOST_TO_NET_U32(capabilities);
1581

1585     mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);

1593     }
1594     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1595   }

asciichat_error_t threaded_send_image_frame (const void *
  pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height, uint32_t
  pixel_format)


Thread-safe image frame transmission. Sends image frames with
    serialization via g_send_mutex to prevent race conditions when multiple
    threads write to the same TCP socket.




Parameters


pixel_data Pixel data buffer


width Frame width in pixels


height Frame height in pixels


pixel_format Pixel format (1=RGB24)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1334 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1335                                                                    {
1336   if (!pixel_data) {
1337     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Pixel data is NULL");
1338   }
1339 
1340   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1341 
1342   // Get transport reference - may be NULL if connection was lost
1343   acip_transport_t *transport = server_connection_get_transport();
1344   if (!transport || !server_connection_is_active()) {
1345     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1346     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Transport unavailable");
1347   }
1348 
1349   // Send image frame with socket lock held
1350   asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_image_frame(transport, pixel_data, width, height, pixel_format);
1351 
1352   // Unlock after send completes
1353   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1354 
1355   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1356   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1357     server_connection_lost();
1358     return result;
1359   }
1360 
1361   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1362 }

    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_NETWORK, mutex_lock,
    mutex_unlock, server_connection_get_transport(),
    server_connection_is_active(), server_connection_lost(), and
    SET_ERRNO.

  void * pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height)


Send H.265-encoded video frame to server (thread-safe). Encodes
    RGB frame using H.265 codec before transmission. Much more
    bandwidth-efficient than raw pixel transmission. Uses mutex to prevent
    interleaving with other packet sends.

pixel_data RGB pixel data from camera


width Frame width in pixels


height Frame height in pixels


Returns

asciichat_error_t threaded_send_packet
  (packet_type_t type, const void * data, size_t len)


Thread-safe packet transmission. Sends a packet to the server with
    proper mutex protection and connection state checking. Automatically handles
    encryption if crypto is ready.




Parameters


type Packet type identifier


data Packet payload


len Payload length


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1136 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1136                                                                                          {
1137   // Lock mutex for entire send operation to prevent concurrent socket writes
1138   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1139 
1140   // Check connection status and get transport reference
1141   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) || !g_client_transport) {
1142     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] threaded_send_packet() check failed: active=%s, transport=%p",
1143               atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) ? "true" : "false", (void *)g_client_transport);
1144     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1145     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection not active or transport unavailable");
1146   }
1147 
1148   // Get transport reference - transport has its own internal synchronization
1149   acip_transport_t *transport = g_client_transport;
1150   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] threaded_send_packet() using transport=%p, is_connected=%s",
1151                   (void *)transport, acip_transport_is_connected(transport) ? "true" : "false");
1152 
1153   // Network I/O happens while holding mutex to prevent concurrent socket writes
1154   asciichat_error_t result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, type, data, len, 0);
1155 
1156   // Unlock after send completes
1157   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1158 
1159   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1160   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1161     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] threaded_send_packet() send failed, calling server_connection_lost()");
1162     server_connection_lost();
1163     return result;
1164   }
1165 
1166   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1167 }

  threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect (unsigned short width, unsigned
  short height)


Thread-safe terminal size packet transmission with auto-detection.
    Sends terminal capabilities packet to the server including terminal size,
    color capabilities, and rendering preferences. Auto-detects terminal
    capabilities if not explicitly specified.




Parameters


width Terminal width in characters


height Terminal height in characters


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1468 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1468                                                                                                             {
1469   // Log the dimensions being sent to server (helps debug dimension mismatch issues)
1470   log_debug("Sending terminal size to server: %ux%u (auto_width=%d, auto_height=%d)", width, height,
1471             GET_OPTION(auto_width), GET_OPTION(auto_height));
1472 
1473   // Connection and transport availability is checked by threaded_send_packet()
1474 
1475   // Build terminal capabilities packet locally
1476   // Detect terminal capabilities automatically
1477   terminal_capabilities_t caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
1478 
1479   // Set wants_padding based on snapshot mode and TTY status
1480   // Disable padding when:
1481   // - In snapshot mode (one frame and exit)
1482   // - When stdout is not a TTY (piped/redirected output)
1483   // Enable padding for interactive terminal sessions
1484   bool is_snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
1485   bool is_interactive = terminal_is_interactive();
1486   caps.wants_padding = is_interactive && !is_snapshot_mode;
1487 
1488   log_debug("Client capabilities: wants_padding=%d (snapshot=%d, interactive=%d, stdin_tty=%d, stdout_tty=%d)",
1489             caps.wants_padding, is_snapshot_mode, is_interactive, terminal_is_stdin_tty(), terminal_is_stdout_tty());
1490 
1491   // Apply user's color mode override
1492   caps = apply_color_mode_override(caps);
1493 
1494   // Check if detection was reliable, use fallback only for auto-detection
1495   if (!caps.detection_reliable && GET_OPTION(color_mode) == COLOR_MODE_AUTO) {
1496     log_warn("Terminal capability detection not reliable, using fallback");
1497     SAFE_MEMSET(&caps, sizeof(caps), 0, sizeof(caps));
1498     caps.color_level = TERM_COLOR_NONE;
1499     caps.color_count = 2;
1500     caps.capabilities = 0;
1501     SAFE_STRNCPY(caps.term_type, "unknown", sizeof(caps.term_type));
1502     SAFE_STRNCPY(caps.colorterm, "", sizeof(caps.colorterm));
1503     caps.detection_reliable = 0;
1504     // Preserve wants_padding even in fallback mode
1505     caps.wants_padding = is_interactive && !is_snapshot_mode;
1506   }
1507 
1508   // Convert to network packet format with proper byte order
1509   terminal_capabilities_packet_t net_packet;
1510   net_packet.capabilities = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.capabilities);
1511   net_packet.color_level = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.color_level);
1512   net_packet.color_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.color_count);
1513   net_packet.render_mode = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.render_mode);
1514   net_packet.width = HOST_TO_NET_U16(width);
1515   net_packet.height = HOST_TO_NET_U16(height);
1516   net_packet.palette_type = HOST_TO_NET_U32(GET_OPTION(palette_type));
1517   net_packet.utf8_support = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.utf8_support ? 1 : 0);
1518 
1519   const options_t *opts = options_get();
1520   if (GET_OPTION(palette_type) == PALETTE_CUSTOM && GET_OPTION(palette_custom_set)) {
1521     const char *palette_custom = opts && opts->palette_custom_set ? opts->palette_custom : "";
1522     SAFE_STRNCPY(net_packet.palette_custom, palette_custom, sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom));
1523     net_packet.palette_custom[sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom) - 1] = '\0';
1524   } else {
1525     SAFE_MEMSET(net_packet.palette_custom, sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom), 0, sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom));
1526   }
1527 
1528   // Set desired FPS
1529   int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
1530   if (fps > 0) {
1531     net_packet.desired_fps = (uint8_t)(fps > 144 ? 144 : fps);
1532   } else {
1533     net_packet.desired_fps = caps.desired_fps;
1534   }
1535 
1536   if (net_packet.desired_fps == 0) {
1537     net_packet.desired_fps = DEFAULT_MAX_FPS;
1538   }
1539 
1540   SAFE_STRNCPY(net_packet.term_type, caps.term_type, sizeof(net_packet.term_type));
1541   net_packet.term_type[sizeof(net_packet.term_type) - 1] = '\0';
1542 
1543   SAFE_STRNCPY(net_packet.colorterm, caps.colorterm, sizeof(net_packet.colorterm));
1544   net_packet.colorterm[sizeof(net_packet.colorterm) - 1] = '\0';
1545 
1546   net_packet.detection_reliable = caps.detection_reliable;
1547   // Send UTF-8 support flag: true for AUTO (default) and TRUE settings, false for FALSE setting
1548   net_packet.utf8_support = (GET_OPTION(force_utf8) != UTF8_SETTING_FALSE) ? 1 : 0;
1549 
1550   // Set wants_padding flag (1=padding enabled, 0=no padding for snapshot/piped modes)
1551   net_packet.wants_padding = caps.wants_padding ? 1 : 0;
1552 
1553   // Set codec capabilities (client supports all codecs by default)
1554   net_packet.codec_capabilities_video = HOST_TO_NET_U32(VIDEO_CODEC_CAP_ALL);
1555   net_packet.codec_capabilities_audio = HOST_TO_NET_U32(AUDIO_CODEC_CAP_ALL);
1556 
1557   // Use threaded_send_packet() which handles encryption
1558   return threaded_send_packet(PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES, &net_packet, sizeof(net_packet));
1559 }

crypto_handshake_context_t g_crypto_ctx = {0}


Per-connection crypto handshake context. Global crypto handshake
    context for this client connection.




Maintains the cryptographic state for the current connection,
    including key exchange state, encryption keys, and handshake progress.




Note


This is not static because it may be accessed from

h265_encoder_t* volatile g_h265_encoder = NULL


Global H.265 encoder context for client video streaming. Encodes
    captured RGB frames to H.265/HEVC format before transmission. Initialized in
    client_main() and used by capture thread. Set to NULL during shutdown
    to prevent use-after-free.
